TSLA: Tesla’s Energy Division Gets UK License to Supply Electricity. Tesla Inc.'s energy division, Tesla Energy Ventures Ltd., has secured a license to supply electricity in the UK. This approval, granted by regulator Ofgem after a seven-month process, allows Tesla to offer its energy services to homes and businesses across Great Britain. The license became effective on Wednesday evening, marking a significant expansion for Elon Musk's company in the energy sector.

Tesla set to start supplying UK homes with electricity. Tesla has been granted a license to supply electricity to British homes, entering the market amidst concerns over rising energy bills. The company's unit, Tesla Energy Ventures, received approval following a process that began last July, positioning Tesla to compete with existing suppliers by leveraging its solar energy and battery storage technology. This move comes as energy prices have surged due to global conflicts and sales of Tesla's electric cars in Britain have declined in recent years.

LiveOne Announces Tesla Subscription Pricing. LiveOne, formerly Slacker Radio, has launched Tesla-exclusive subscription pricing after its free agreement with Tesla lapsed. Users can choose between LiveOne Plus ($3.99/month or $34.99/year) which offers ad-free radio stations, or LiveOne Premium ($9.99/month or $99.99/year) which adds on-demand listening and offline downloads, although these advanced features are not yet supported in Teslas. The article highlights that users can continue LiveOne for free with ads and limited skips after December 1st, 2024.

Lucid sees a future with a robotaxi business and thriving mid-size EV platform (LCID:NASDAQ). Lucid Group announced plans for a new mid-size EV platform that will support three new vehicles targeting the core EV market. The company also indicated intentions to enter the robotaxi market through partnerships. Lucid aims for a ~$50K starting price for its new models, emphasizing range, efficiency, and space to compete with Tesla's Model 3 and Y.

Tesla's FSD Safety Metrics 'Sharply Deteriorating,' Says Analyst. An analyst from GLJ Research, Gordon Johnson, claims that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) safety metrics are "sharply deteriorating" based on third-party Community Tracker data. The data indicates a significant drop in "city miles to critical disengagement" after the FSD v14.2 rollout. Johnson also highlights a large discrepancy between Tesla's FSD performance and Waymo's, raising questions about NHTSA's oversight.

Did GM Just Use The EV1 To Troll Ford, Again? Or Tesla? Or Both?. General Motors is using the restoration of its vintage EV1 electric car to highlight its long-standing commitment to EVs and to subtly critique Ford and Tesla. The article points out that GM is re-launching the Chevy Bolt at a more affordable price than Tesla's entry-level Model 3 and emphasizes GM's pioneering role in EV technology through the EV1. It also touches on advancements in battery technology and GM's future EV plans.

Tesla VP explains latest updates in trade secret theft case. Tesla Vice President Bonne Eggleston provided an update on the company's trade secret theft lawsuit against former supplier Matthews International. A judge issued a permanent injunction blocking Matthews from using certain stolen Tesla parts or designs and found them liable for damages, which will be calculated later. Tesla initiated the suit, demanding over $1 billion, after allegedly finding Matthews copying proprietary battery manufacturing technology and selling it to competitors despite earlier denials.

Musk unveils joint Tesla-xAI project 'Macrohard', eyes software disruption. Elon Musk has announced a joint project between Tesla and xAI called "Macrohard" or "Digital Optimus," which he claims can emulate the functions of entire software companies. This system combines xAI's Grok large language model with a Tesla-developed AI agent to process real-time computer screen input and user actions. The initiative comes amidst concerns among investors about agents-based AI disrupting established business models and follows recent significant investments in and acquisitions of xAI by Tesla and SpaceX.
TGT: ULTA: Evercore ISI Group Raises Price Target to $725, Maintains Outperform Rating | ULTA Stock News. Evercore ISI Group has raised its price target for Ulta Beauty (ULTA) from $660.00 to $725.00, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a 9.85% increase in the price target. Other analysts have also recently updated their ratings and price targets for ULTA, with an average target price of $690.11 among 23 analysts.
SBUX: Starbucks, Amgen, CVS and Other Stocks Help This Income Fund Shine. Hilda Applbaum, co-manager of the American Funds Income Fund of America, employs a strategy of investing in inexpensive, high-yielding stocks with potential for growth. This approach has led to her fund outperforming 98% of its peers. The article highlights how stocks like Starbucks, Amgen, and CVS contribute to the fund's success.

Starbucks Founder Leaves Washington State With Millionaire Tax Rattling the Rich. Starbucks Corp. founder Howard Schultz has announced his departure from Washington state to move to Miami, citing family reasons after more than four decades. His move comes as Washington state lawmakers are actively debating a proposed "millionaire's tax," which has caused concern within the Seattle business community. Although Schultz did not explicitly mention taxes, his departure coincides with the impending enactment of the state's first income levy.

How to watch Starbucks' 2026 virtual shareholder meeting. Starbucks Coffee Company (NASDAQ: SBUX) will hold its Annual Meeting of Shareholders virtually on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Pacific Time. Shareholders on record as of January 16, 2026, are eligible to vote and can access the meeting via investor.starbucks.com. A replay of the meeting will be available on the company’s website until October 28, 2026.
DIS: Bokf Na Trims Position in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Bokf Na reduced its holdings in The Walt Disney Company (DIS) by 5% in the third quarter, selling 8,416 shares and retaining 160,662 shares valued at approximately $18.4 million. Despite this reduction, other large institutions like Norges Bank and Viking Global made significant increases in their holdings, with institutional investors collectively owning about 65.71% of Disney's stock. Analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Disney with an average price target of $135.80, following the company's recent beat on Q1 EPS and revenue estimates.

Paul Roeder Named Chief Communications Officer Of The Walt Disney Company. Paul Roeder, a 25-year Disney veteran, has been appointed Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer of The Walt Disney Company, effective March 19, reporting directly to incoming CEO Josh D’Amaro. Roeder will lead Disney’s global communications strategy, overseeing media relations, executive communications, public affairs, and corporate social responsibility. He previously headed communications for Disney Entertainment Studios, Direct-to-Consumer, and International, playing a key role in major acquisitions and film releases.

What's in Your Stomach? Jennifer Garner Celebrates Once Upon a Farm Products at Disney California Adventure. Jennifer Garner's baby food brand, Once Upon a Farm, is now available at Disney California Adventure, specifically at Fillmore's Taste-In in Cars Land. Garner celebrated this milestone at the Disney California Adventure Food & Wine Festival, where her brand also sponsors a special shaded picnic area. This area will be available until April 27, 2026.

Disney 2026 shareholder meeting goes live in March 18 webcast. The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS) will webcast its annual shareholder meeting live on March 18, 2026, starting at 1:00 p.m. ET/10:00 a.m. PT. The event will include remarks from management and will be accessible via the company's investor relations website. An archived version of the webcast will also be available.
